skill.name: github-issue-creator
skill.description: Convert raw notes, error logs, voice dictation, or screenshots into crisp GitHub-flavored markdown issue reports.
skill.summary: Transform messy input into clean, actionable GitHub issues with structured templates.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 21 patterns as potential security issues, but all are false positives. The SKILL.md file contains only markdown documentation and prompt templates - no executable code. Backtick characters are used for markdown code blocks and inline code formatting, not shell command execution. No cryptographic functions or external command execution present.

content.user_title: Create GitHub Issues from Raw Notes
content.value_statement: Stop wrestling with unstructured bug reports. Transform voice notes, error logs, and messy descriptions into professional GitHub issues that developers can actually use.
actual_capabilities: Parse unstructured voice dictation and extract key bug details
Format error logs into structured error sections
Generate complete GitHub-flavored markdown issue templates
Infer missing context from conversation history
Categorize issue severity based on impact description
Create organized markdown files in repository issues directory
limitations: Cannot directly create issues on GitHub - generates markdown files only
Requires user to provide initial bug description or error information
Cannot test or verify reported bugs independently
Cannot access external systems to validate environment details

use_cases.title: Quick Bug Documentation
use_cases.description: Dictate bug details while reproducing an issue, then have them instantly formatted as a proper GitHub issue.

use_cases.title: Error Log Analysis
use_cases.description: Paste raw error messages and stack traces to generate structured issue reports with proper formatting.

use_cases.title: Incident Response
use_cases.description: Document production incidents quickly by describing what happened, then generate formal issue reports for tracking.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Error Report
prompt_templates.prompt: I got this error: [paste error message]. Create a GitHub issue for this bug.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick bug report from error message

prompt_templates.title: Voice Dictation
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this voice note into a GitHub issue: [paste transcribed voice message describing a bug]

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Incident
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub issue from this incident: Product: [name], Error: [details], Steps: [what you did], Expected: [what should happen], Impact: [who is affected]

prompt_templates.title: Regression Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Document this regression as a GitHub issue: Feature [name] was working [when]. Now [current behavior]. Error: [any error messages]. This blocks [workflow].

output_examples.input: Error: PERMISSION_DENIED when publishing to Teams channel. Code: 403. Was working yesterday.
output: Summary: 403 PERMISSION_DENIED error when publishing to Teams channel
Expected: Agent publishes successfully to Teams channel
Impact: High - Blocks Teams integration, regression from previous working state

output_examples.input: so I was trying to deploy the agent and it just failed silently no error nothing the workflow ran but then poof gone from the list had to refresh and try again three times
output: Summary: Agent deployment fails silently - no error displayed, agent disappears from list
Reproduction: Deploy agent, observe workflow completes, check agent list
Expected: Agent appears in list with deployment status
Actual: Agent disappears from list with no error message
Impact: High - Blocks agent deployment workflow, no feedback on failure cause

best_practices: Include specific error codes and messages in the Error Details section for faster debugging
Clearly separate expected vs actual behavior to help developers understand the gap
Assign severity levels honestly based on user impact, not personal frustration
anti_patterns: Vague descriptions like it does not work without specific details about what is broken
Mixing multiple unrelated bugs in one issue - create separate issues instead
Speculating about root causes in the issue body instead of sticking to observed facts

faq.question: Does this skill create issues directly on GitHub?
faq.answer: No, it generates markdown files in your repository issues directory. You can review and manually create the GitHub issue or use other tools to automate the upload.

faq.question: What if I do not have all the environment details?
faq.answer: The skill uses placeholders like PROJECT_NAME or REGION for missing information. Fill these in when you know them.

faq.question: Can it analyze my code to verify the bug?
faq.answer: No, this skill formats reported issues but does not test, debug, or verify code behavior.

faq.question: How does it determine severity levels?
faq.answer: Severity is based on impact: Critical for service down or data loss, High for blocked features, Medium for impaired functionality with workarounds, Low for minor issues.

faq.question: Can it handle screenshots or images?
faq.answer: It references images inline with placeholder descriptions. You will need to attach actual screenshots separately.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with sensitive code?
faq.answer: Yes, it generates local markdown files and does not send data to external services. Always review generated content before sharing.
